title: Debug a Failing Deployment
description:
## Fix the Broken Deployment

A deployment has been pre-created in the `debug` namespace but its pods are not running.

**Your task:** Identify the problem and fix it so that the deployment has **all 3 pods in Ready state**.

> Start by describing the deployment and its pods to find clues about what's wrong.

hints.title: Inspect the deployment
hints.body: Start by listing pods in the debug namespace and describing the failing ones.
hints.command:
kubectl get pods -n debug
kubectl describe pod -n debug -l app=broken-app

hints.title: Check events
hints.body: The Events section in `kubectl describe` usually tells you exactly what's wrong — look for ImagePullBackOff, OOMKilled, or probe failures.

hints.title: Fix the image tag
hints.body: If the image tag doesn't exist, update it to a valid one using `kubectl set image`.
hints.command: kubectl set image deployment/broken-app nginx=nginx:1.25 -n debug

validation.description: Checks that the broken-app deployment in the debug namespace has 3 ready replicas.

commands.command: kubectl get deployment broken-app -n debug -o jsonpath='{.status.readyReplicas}' 2>/dev/null | grep -v '^$' || echo 0
kubectl get pods -n debug --no-headers 2>/dev/null | awk '{print $3}' | grep -c 'ImagePullBackOff\|ErrImagePull' || true
